user_query:
My local "setlist viewer" server that I use to cue one-drop intros before rehearsals keeps failing to start. Please triage and fix it so it starts reliably on my machine, then document what you did.

What I need you to do from this workspace:
1) Investigate the last failure:
   - Read logs/previous_run.log and extract the exact line that explains why it failed.
   - Attempt to start the server with the current config and capture the error output for evidence:
     - Command to run: python3 app/setlist_server.py --config config/settings.json
     - Save the full stdout+stderr from this pre-fix attempt to out/failed_startup.txt.
2) Remediate the issue by editing the configuration:
   - Modify config/settings.json to keep the same host but change the port to 8081 (the current 8080 is the problem) so the server can bind successfully.
   - While you're in there, add reliability settings for future incidents: add fields "auto_restart": true and "max_restarts": 2 to config/settings.json. (These are for future use and should remain in the file.)
3) Verify the fix:
   - Start the server again after the config change using the same command:
     - Command: python3 app/setlist_server.py --config config/settings.json
   - Save the full stdout+stderr from this post-fix run to out/server_startup.txt.
   - The run is considered successful if out/server_startup.txt contains a line that starts with "Serving setlist viewer on" and the process exits cleanly.
4) Deliver a short incident report at out/incident_report.md with these sections:
   - root_cause: quote the exact single line from logs/previous_run.log that indicates the failure cause.
   - command_output: include the relevant error line from out/failed_startup.txt that matches the failure.
   - fix: describe the config change you made and include the updated host/port JSON snippet.
   - verification: include the success line from out/server_startup.txt proving it starts.

Notes:
- Do not change the host value.
- Do not add new files outside the out/ directory except modifying config/settings.json.
- All referenced paths are relative to this workspace. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
